t1: Iranfar et al. (2026) rescued hearing in DFNB16 mice using dual-AAV: ~60% of outer hair cells got both vectors. But mice have 3.5 mm cochleas with ~3,300 OHCs. Humans have 35 mm cochleas with ~12,000 OHCs. Our Poisson stochastic model predicts a dramatic drop:
t2: A single-vector approach eliminates the co-transduction bottleneck entirely. Every cell that gets the vector gets the full gene.
t3: 2. Why dual-vector fails at human scale
t6: Poisson model prediction at human scale
